baseline.profile_rank_notes: Single-equation safe/default Add/Mul literal cases were the smallest egraph calls still paying full egg setup.

after.command: RCH_FORCE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/frankenjax-fj-egraph-profile-pass23-after cargo bench -p fj-egraph --bench egraph_baseline -- --sample-size 40 --measurement-time 2 --warm-up-time 1

lever.description: For a safe/default one-equation Add or Mul segment with exactly one literal and one variable input, emit the existing literal-first commutative canonical equation directly instead of building/running egg.
lever.scope: Only numerical_safety_mode=true, single-output, param-free, effect-free, sub-jaxpr-free Add/Mul equations. Aggressive-mode reductions and all other segments continue through the existing egraph path.

proof: The fast path does not reduce x+0 or x*1 in safe/default mode; it only emits the same literal-first commutative form already produced by extraction for these single-literal Add/Mul cases.
Output VarId is preserved and outvar_remap is identity for all segment outvars.
It is gated off for aggressive mode, multi-equation segments, multi-output equations, params, effects, and sub-jaxprs.
The focused golden test evaluates both original and optimized programs at f64 3.5 and asserts identical Value output, including dtype.
No RNG is involved; no floating-point operation order changes because the equation remains a single Add or Mul with the same operands and output.
